q: ___ is the form of energy that can be transferred from one system to another as a result of temperature difference.
Heat transfer is a discipline of ___ engineering.
The three basic ways of heat transfer are heat conduction, heat convection, and ___.
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by ___, in which the motion of the fluid itself carries heat from one place to another.
In solid, heat is transferred by ___ process.
The third way to transfer heat energy is by radiation process in the form of ___.
The individual parts of a medium do not move as a whole in ___.
Successive distribution of heat energy among the ___ parts occurs in heat conduction.
The thermal conductivity of a material is a measure of the ___ of a material to conduct heat.
In SI units, the unit of thermal conductivity $k$ is ___.
The ratio $\\frac{T_2-T_1}{l}$ is called ___.
___ law states that the rate of heat flow per unit area is proportional to the magnitude of temperature gradient.
Heat current $H$ is measured in ___ in SI units.
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates that the material is a good ___.
Materials such as rubber, wood, and styrofoam are ___ of heat.
Heat convection is the flow of heat through a ___ from places of higher temperature to places of lower temperature.
The heat is carried by a ___ fluid from one place to another in convection.
Hotter fluid rises while cooler and denser fluid ___.
The basic equation for the rate of convection heat transfer is known as ___ law of cooling.
The quantity $q$ in the convection equation is called the ___ coefficient.
In cold countries, the hot water rises to the top of a tank in a household hot water system due to ___.
Heat radiation is a transfer of heat that does not require a ___.
Energy transfer by radiation is the fastest as it occurs at the ___.
The energy of the sun reaches the earth by ___.
Thermal radiation is the form of radiation emitted by bodies because of their ___.
All solids, liquids and gases emit or absorb radiation to varying ___.
A black object is a good ___ and a good radiator.
The ___ of an object depends on the wavelength of radiation.
The object which can absorb all the electromagnetic radiations falling upon it is called a ___.
Radiation emitted by a blackbody is called ___ radiation.
___ is the study of systems involving energy in the form of heat and work.
___ is an important concept in thermodynamics which occurs when systems at the same temperature have no net heat transfer.
The ___ law of thermodynamics states that when two objects are separately in thermodynamic equilibrium with a third object, they all are in thermodynamic equilibrium with each other.
The first law of thermodynamics relates the change in ___ of a system to heat added and work done.
The first law of thermodynamics is an application of the ___ for heat and thermodynamic processes.
The internal energy $U$ of a system is defined as the sum of the ___ of all the constituent particles.
Internal energy of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the ___.
Neither heat nor work is ___ as such in a system.
A thermodynamic process may be defined as a change from one ___ of a system to another.
A quasi-static process is also known as a ___ process.
An ___ process occurs at constant pressure.
In an isobaric process, the work done is equal to the area of the ___ on a pressure vs volume graph.
An ___ process occurs at constant volume.
Isochoric process is also known as ___ process.
In an isochoric process, the change in internal energy must equal any ___ flowing into or out of the system.
An ___ process occurs at a constant temperature.
In an isothermal process for an ideal gas, the change in internal energy is equal to ___.
In an ___ process, there is no heat transfer into or out of the system.
The first law of thermodynamics for an adiabatic process is reduced to ___.
In an adiabatic process, the system is enclosed in a ___.
A ___ is a system that converts heat to mechanical energy.
A series of processes in which a system is brought back to its initial state is called a ___.
Thermal efficiency of a heat engine is the ratio of ___ to heat input.
The transformation of heat into work is usually accomplished in practice by internal combustion engines and ___.
The two types of internal combustion engines are gasoline engine and ___ engine.
The cycle of a gasoline engine involves the performance of ___ processes.
In gasoline engines, the mixture of gasoline vapour and air is compressed during the ___ stroke.
During the ___ stroke of a gasoline engine, hot combustion products expand and push the piston out.
Unlike the gasoline engine, the diesel engine induces only ___ into the combustion chamber on its intake stroke.
The diesel engine induces ignition by spraying fuel into a cylinder containing highly ___ air.
In 1824, Nicolas Leonard Sadi Carnot described a hypothetical engine known as ___ engine.
Carnot engine operates on an ideal ___ cycle.
A ___ process is one where every point along the path is in an equilibrium state.
The working substance of a Carnot engine is an ___.
The Carnot cycle consists of two isothermal processes and two ___ processes.
During reversible isothermal expansion in the Carnot cycle, the gas causes the piston to do work on the ___.
Reversible isothermal compression in the Carnot cycle involves heat ___ to a low reservoir.
Carnot's theorem states that no engine operating between two heat reservoirs can be more ___ than a Carnot engine.
All reversible engines operating between the same heat reservoirs are ___ efficient.
